lines.text: I need one on the books by Saturday, Pell. [pause] A real one, serial number and all. You miss it, they fold your office into Lincoln and I quit taking your calls.
(warm) Mr. Dawes. Morning. [pause] And thank you, real sincerely, for your patience with all this.
They built these to last, didn't they. [warmly] You'd pay a fortune for joinery like this over on the continent.
It was surplus. Nineteen seventy-five. [pause] Told it'd be scrapped otherwise.
(quiet and hard) [quietly] Not one of you ever built a thing out here. [pause] Now it's all yours.

You didn't build this either, Mr. Dawes. Nineteen seventy-five it should've gone back to NASA, and it never did, [pause] and for fifty years nobody so much as asked. You just had it a long time. Long enough it started to feel like yours.
[quietly] You'll have to move me, Wade.
(low) Earl. Federal list came down Tuesday. [pause] County don't see a dime off any of it. Nobody does.
(to Stitch) Warehouse outside Lincoln, is what I hear. Same one as the rest of it.
(to Cricket) There's a window to appeal it. [pause] It's open till four.
Folks, I know change feels sudden. [pause] But as the Accord reminds us — harmony is a verb.
